chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَحِلُّ لِلرَّجُلِ مِنِ امْرَأَتِهِ وَ هِيَ طَامِثٌ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 176 – What is Permissible for the man, from his woman, and she is menstruating

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (0/382)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (0/382)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) تَرَى هَؤُلَاءِ الْمُشَوَّهِينَ خَلْقُهُمْ قَالَ قُلْتُ نَعَمْ قَالَ هَؤُلَاءِ الَّذِينَ آبَاؤُهُمْ يَأْتُونَ نِسَاءَهُمْ فِي الطَّمْثِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Al Hassan Bin Atiyya, from Uzafir Al Sayrafi who said,
‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Do you see these disfigured ones?’ I said, ‘Yes’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘They are those who fathers were going to their wives during the menstruation’.
en.sarwar: 5. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from al-Hassan ibn ‘Atiyyah from ‘Adhafir al-Sayrafiy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, asked, ‘Do you see those who are born with deformities?’ I replied, ‘Yes, I see them.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘It is because their fathers go to bed with their wives during their experiencing Hayd (menses).”’
